channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dadu Court Acquits All Accused in Chandio Family Murder Case After Seven-Year Trial
item.description: A local court in Dadu has acquitted all eight accused in the long-running and highly publicized murder case involving the family of Umme Rubab Chandio. The case, which dates back several years, result...

item.title: Kairo Forbes recognised for dedication and growth in modelling at 2026 awards
item.description: Young South African model Kairo Forbes has made headlines after winning both Junior Model of the Year 2026 and Junior Judges’ Choice Overall. The awards celebrate her steady progress, confidence, and ...

item.title: Main News Stories Across Nigeria on March 30, 2026
item.description: Naija News give rundown of major gist wey dey trend across Nigeria on March 30, 2026. President Bola Tinubu mark him 74th birthday by donating all the 34 months salary wey he don collect since May 202...

item.title: SA government to cushion households as fuel prices are set to rise amid global tensions
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has instructed Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana to develop measures to cushion South Africans from an anticipated fuel price increase slated for April 1. The move follows ri...

item.title: Luke Newton and Nicola Coughlan: Great On-Screen Chemistry, Just Friends Off-Screen
item.description: Luke Newton and Nicola Coughlan, stars of Bridgerton season 3, have sparked dating rumours thanks to their strong on-screen chemistry as Colin Bridgerton and Penelope Featherington. Fans have been cur...

item.title: Five civilians killed, six militants neutralised in coordinated Panjgur house attacks; Quetta also hit by grenade assaults
item.description: Eleven people, including six alleged terrorists, were killed in coordinated attacks on multiple houses in Panjgur district, Balochistan, while one person was killed and 12 others injured in separate g...

item.title: Impact of Iran Conflict on Pakistan’s Fertiliser Supply and Food Security
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran has significantly affected global oil, gas, and fertiliser markets, primarily due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz — a key route for energy exports. Because natural gas i...

item.title: Joni Mitchell receives lifetime achievement Juno at 2026 awards ceremony
item.description: The 2026 Juno Awards honored Joni Mitchell with a lifetime achievement award during the gala at the TD Coliseum in Hamilton. Mitchell, 82, accepted the award with the help of a cane, reflecting on how...

item.title: How Gulf tensions are worsening Pakistan’s cost-of-living and workplace pressures
item.description: This article explains how the current conflict in the Gulf is not only disturbing oil markets and global trade, but also quietly pushing up the cost of living in Pakistan and reshaping the labour mark...

item.title: Siwelele owner Calvin Le John faces lawsuit over alleged sponsorship commission
item.description: Siwelele, the PSL club owned by Calvin Le John, is entangled in a court dispute over an alleged sponsorship commission. Flash Rights Sales claims it facilitated sponsorship deals totaling about R10 mi...

item.title: Nkosinathi Joyi Retains IBF Strawweight Title in 2012 Highlight Featured in SA Sports History (March 30)
item.description: In a feature tracing notable moments in South African sport history for March 30, the article spotlights Nkosinathi Joyi retaining the IBF strawweight title against Katsunari Takayama at Emperors Pala...

item.title: Pakistan’s Auto Sector Urgently Needs a Localisation-Focused Policy Reform
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan’s growing dependence on imported completely and semi-knockdown (CKD/SKD) automobile kits despite years of government policies aimed at promoting localisation. According ...

item.title: Fuel Price Surge Threatens South African Economy and Households
item.description: South Africa is bracing for historic fuel price hikes from 1 April, with petrol expected to rise by more than R5 a litre, diesel by R10, and paraffin by R11. The sharp increases, linked to turmoil in ...

item.title: UNEP warns that global food waste threatens climate goals and food security
item.description: The United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) has highlighted a staggering global issue: more than one billion tonnes of edible food are wasted each year, representing roughly one-fifth of all food ...

item.title: PM Modi accuses Kerala’s main fronts of mismanagement, says BJP aims for clean governance
item.description: During an election rally in Palakkad ahead of the 2026 Kerala assembly polls, Prime Minister Narendra Modi declared that the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)-led National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is the ...

item.title: Pakistan offers to host US‑Iran talks as quad ministers push for negotiated end to Gulf conflict
item.description: Pakistan nehnay ko signal diyaa hai ke wah future mein United States aur Iran ke darmiyan seedhi baat‑cheet ki mizaab (host) ban sakta hai, jab Deputy Prime Minister aur Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar ne ...

item.title: Indus River Authority Faces Hurdles Ahead of Crucial Water Management Meeting
item.description: Pakistan's Indus River System Authority (IRSA) is grappling with significant challenges as it prepares for a vital meeting focused on water management. The authority, responsible for coordinating wate...

item.title: PM assures sufficient fuel reserves amid Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif stated that Pakistan holds adequate petroleum reserves to meet national requirements despite the global oil market disruptions caused by the ongoing US-Israel conflict wi...

item.title: Trump’s Cuba remarks and Cuba’s enduring solidarity: a reflective piece
item.description: The article discusses how US President Donald Trump’s casual rhetoric about “taking Cuba” sparked global conversations, emphasizing Cuba’s historical support for communities under aggression and occup...

item.title: Middle East tensions threaten Pakistan’s remittance and job stability
item.description: Pakistan’s economy is currently navigating a stabilisation phase, but rising instability in the Middle East poses a major threat to two critical economic lifelines: overseas employment and workers’ re...
